74044.subject: PFI Monthly - May Edition
74044.body:
PFI Monthly 

Автор: Prague Finance Institute


Автор: Prague Finance Institute

    
    
    Прочитайте цю статтю на LinkedIn, щоб приєднатися до обговорення
Прочитати на LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/pfi-monthly-may-edition-prague-finance-institute-6lqvf?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3BD61o8EPuTCqqxsY8x9mV%2Fg%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=3j3tL4gM2iQIg1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-fz96fz~mpzdu0ez~g7-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mpzdu0ez-g7
  
Dear professionals, welcome to the new edition of our PFI newsletter. In the March issue, we have prepared for you: PFI Monthly research report PFI Roundtable PFI Talks PFI Monthly In this edition, we cover topics from financial markets, macroeconomics, corporate finance, private equity, banking, behavioral finance, and the Czech M&A market. The May edition also includes our interview of the month with Tomáš Pfeiler, CFA, discussing the current AI-driven market environment, equity valuations, interest rates, government bonds, private credit, and broader risks across global financial markets. In this edition: Interview of the month – Tomáš Pfeiler, CFA: AI euphoria is the prevailing market narrative once again Trade-weighted real effective exchange rate (REER) as an essential analytical concept for every economist Simple, not easy: Every stock return has just two moving parts The return of the discount rate Equity investing 101 – Investors have to distinguish between business performance and market pricing Efficient market hypothesis (EMH) and expectations investing Capital allocation – The CEO’s most important job Seyfor enters CRM with 40% stake in Raynet at close to CZK 1bn valuation Private equity plays the long game Private equity is financing liquidity What covered bond pricing reveals about the Czech market Prospect theory – The intellectual foundation of behavioral finance Book review of the month – Mastering the Market Cycle: Getting the Odds on Your Side by Howard Marks 👉 Read the full edition to explore all insights in detail. PFI ROUNDTABLE | The ideal EXIT through the eyes of legal advisors and private equity investors What does a successful investment exit look like from the perspective of investors and legal advisors? And what is everything that goes into successfully completing a transaction? At the next PFI Roundtable meeting, we will open one of the key topics in the world of private equity and M&A – the ideal EXIT. Insights from major transactions and investments will be shared by leading experts from practice: ◾️ Jana Chwaszcz, Local Partner, White & Case LLP ◾️ Petr Rieger, Director, The Carlyle Group ◾️ Jan Černý, Managing Partner, Pale Fire Capital Moderator: Petr Skocdopole After the discussion, there will be informal networking. 📍Wednesday, June 10, 2026 I 18:00 Zenwork Palác ARA Registration here! C

74042.subject: Team1 Applications Are Open: Build The Future Of Avalanche
74042.body:
View this post on the web at https://www.team1.blog/p/team1-applications-are-open-build

Team1 has spent the past few years doing the ground-level work of making Avalanche more accessible. 450+ members across 40 countries, 6 official chapters, 600+ community events, 75,000+ Luma subscribers growing 15% month over month, and collaborations with 130 universities. Six country chapters are live, with at least 6 more set to launch before the end of 2026.
Now, Team1 is looking for the next group to grow the Avalanche ecosystem.
Applications opened May 27th and close June 16th.
Why This Moment
Avalanche’s ecosystem is moving fast, and the gaps where community support makes a real difference are getting harder to ignore. New L1s are launching, developer tooling is maturing, and the user base is expanding into regions that are just starting to find their footing. Team1 is growing its core global team and rolling out 12+ new regional chapters this year.
The people who join now will shape what that expansion looks like.
Want to keep up with the latest in the Avalanche ecosystem? Subscribe to the Team1 blog and get our updates in your inbox weekly.
What Team1 Actually Does
Team1 is a global community built around growing the Avalanche ecosystem through education, events, content, and direct support for builders and newcomers. Members work in their local regions and across time zones to help people understand Avalanche, establish a presence, and join the ecosystem.
What that looks like varies. Some members run hackathons or university workshops. Others write guides, produce content, or help onboard developers and founders. Some show up through Discord, X, regional calls, or by connecting builders with the right people at the right time. Team1 supports all of it: event funding pathways, content bounty opportunities, creative support, and direct connections across the ecosystem.
It’s real work that impacts the ecosystem.
The Kind of Person Team1 Is Looking For
Team1 is built for people who show up consistently in ways that fit their schedule and their strengths. You don’t need to be a full-time Web3 professional or someone who can host events every week. Whether you are a founder, event host, developer, graphic designer, videographer, or business development professional, we are looking for people who bring their unique skills to the table.
Integrity matters most. Members represent the community, and that carries weight. Beyond that, Team1 looks for people who take initiative without being asked, who are willing to try something new even when it doesn’t land perfectly, and who want to help others navigate the ecosystem rather than just building their own profile.
Collaboration is built into how Team1 operates. Members work across regions and countries, share ideas, and support each other’s local efforts. If you find satisfaction in helping something or someone grow, you’ll fit in here.
What You Get Out of It
Team1 gives you the infrastructure

74039.subject: Sam Altman and Anthropic finally agree on something
74039.body:
----------
######                      [Sign Up](http://utm.genai.works/r/gg2ubr5) | [Collaborate with us](https://6hhfxazcbpc.typeform.com/to/uXIl0IFH?utm_source=atlas&utm_medium=newsletter&utm_campaign=general_2026_05_11&utm_content=banner_post)


--------------------
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/8776fa6e-e2d0-4aec-9648-1bed6de3a659/GenAI_Daily_Banner.jpg?t=1778687445)
Caption: 


----------

----------
**Welcome back!** It takes a lot to get OpenAI and Anthropic on the same page and this risk did it. At the same time, Sam Altman was working the halls of Congress in person, Anthropic lined up Wall Street's biggest banks for its IPO and Meta showed off an AI agent that can close sales for a business.

———————————————————————————

**In today's Generative AI Newsletter:**

* **The DNA letter:** Why would rival AI labs ask Congress to regulate them?

* **The 30-day rule: **Who changed what policy makers initially planned for AI?

* **Anthropic's IPO:** What does a $60 billion listing buy Claude users?

* **Meta's agent:** Would you let an AI book appointments and close sales for your business?


----------

----------
## [AI's fiercest rivals ask Congress to intervene](https://www.wsj.com/politics/policy/top-ai-ceos-call-for-law-protecting-against-biological-weapons-88f2f99f)

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/1154c1d8-b4a4-47f0-a0d8-408786185383/Beehiiv_01_-_Jun_4.png?t=1780567145)
Caption: 

The people running the biggest AI companies asked Washington to get involved because AI lowers the bar on building a biological weapon.

**Who?**

* Demis Hassabis of Google DeepMind

* Sam Altman of OpenAI

* Dario Amodei of Anthropic 

* Mustafa Suleyman of Microsoft AI 

They all signed a public letter pushing for new rules.

For decades the thing stopping a bad actor from making a dangerous pathogen was the know-how. Now everyone can access AI so we need to be a little bit concerned.

* **The ask:** Companies that sell synthetic DNA and RNA should be required to screen both the customer and the order. 

* **Why now:** Back in 2017, Canadian researchers used about $100,000 of mail-order DNA to rebuild horsepox, a close cousin of smallpox. Synthesis has only gotten cheaper since.

* **Not so easy:** Screening isn't airtight. Last year Microsoft researchers showed that AI protein-design tools could generate dangerous sequences that slipped right past the screening software companies already use.

* **The bill:** A bipartisan Senate bill from Tom Cotton and Amy Klobuchar would force every gene-synthesis provider in the US to screen orders and customers.

These companies almost never ask Washington to regulate them. Signing this letter says they would rather help write a screening law now than answer for a bioweapo

74037.subject: Stripe, Visa, Mastercard, & Coinbase could launch a stablecoin that strands Circle’s USDC 😳🪙; Ramp just gave acc…
74037.body:
View this post on the web at https://linas.substack.com/p/fintechpulse1086

Hey Everyone,
Good morning & happy Thursday! Today’s issue is incredibly interesting as we’re diving deep into Stripe, Visa, Mastercard, and Coinbase, which are launching a stablecoin that could dethrone Circle’s USDC (what it’s all about, why it could be huge, why the timing is brutal for Circle & why Coinbase can afford this gamble + bonus deep dives into the latest financials of Circle [ https://substack.com/redirect/525babfb-a549-4824-9bc7-dc33f03b453a?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ] & Coinbase [ https://substack.com/redirect/87501caa-04e8-46c2-a316-deb12fd5d2d9?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ], and the ultimate list of resources on stables [ https://substack.com/redirect/3962f92c-2767-4010-a105-de5ca24867e6?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ] inside), and fintech giant Ramp that just gave accountants Free AI to get at their clients (what Ramp Stack is all about & why it’s a brilliant play + bonus playbook on how Ramp is building AI-powered engineering teams and products [ https://substack.com/redirect/a146de27-07a5-4ea3-b0b1-3891eb0e67cb?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ] & The Claude Finance Playbook [ https://substack.com/redirect/1cbe7440-5e0c-45d2-bcb0-dae4e5de7a5c?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ] inside). So let’s jump straight into the paradigm-defining stuff 🌶️
Stripe, Visa, Mastercard, & Coinbase could launch a stablecoin that strands Circle’s USDC 😳🪙
The BIG News 🔥 Nearly a quarter of Coinbase’s $COIN  revenue flows from a single relationship: the USDC deal it struck with Circle $CRCL . Which is why this week's leak cuts so deep. Coinbase may now reportedly be helping Stripe, Visa, and Mastercard to build the stablecoin meant to replace USDC.
Circle’s recently released Q1 earnings [ https://substack.com/redirect/525babfb-a549-4824-9bc7-dc33f03b453a?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ] show why the timing is brutal for Circle, we now know why Coinbase can afford the gamble, and why a token nobody has confirmed is already the most valuable threat in payments.
Let’s dive deeper into this, understand why it matters, and see what to expect next...

Unsubscribe https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9saW5hcy5zdWJzdGFjay5jb20vYWN0aW9uL2Rpc2FibGVfZW1haWw_.Eyry_IZQgpIV6VAsEBsRj2GKFZYAhfZSXrz-HBh9Zp8?
